source.description: Six-panel generated marker contact sheet saved under drafts/red-white-blue-popsicle-badge/ before cropping.
source.notes: Promoted after the owner asked to turn unused generated art into backfill tutorials.

process_strategy: The contact sheet builds one centered popsicle badge from rounded construction shapes through outline, stick, color bands, shine marks, and final marker cleanup.

final_elements.name: rounded popsicle body
final_elements.description: A tall rounded rectangle popsicle shape with softened top corners.

final_elements.name: wooden stick
final_elements.description: A small centered stick below the popsicle body.

final_elements.name: badge outline
final_elements.description: A thick black sticker-like outline around the body and stick.

final_elements.name: three color bands
final_elements.description: Horizontal red, white, and blue sections across the existing body.

final_elements.name: wavy melt edge
final_elements.description: A small wavy drip or melt edge cut into the lower color area.

final_elements.name: shine and marker texture
final_elements.description: Small shine marks and visible marker fill streaks inside existing shapes.

final_elements.name: small sticker shadow
final_elements.description: A soft colored shadow behind the badge shape.

frames.visible_job: Draws the tall rounded popsicle body guide with a soft top, straight sides, and flatter bottom edge.
Adds the centered stick and thickens the body into a badge outline.
Divides the body into red, white, and blue horizontal bands.
Adds a small wavy melt edge and clarifies the band shapes.
Fills existing bands with red and blue marker, leaves the white band open, adds shine marks, marker streaks, and a small sticker shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only reinforces existing black outlines, smooths marker fills, sharpens shine marks, and deepens the small badge shadow.

rejection_checks: No stick, color band, melt edge, shine mark, or shadow should first appear in the final image.
No frame may ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same popsicle angle, scale, and placement as the step frames.
